subject: Social Science (History)
chapter: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947

key_points: Nationalism is the feeling of unity and pride among people of a nation
The Indian National Congress was formed in 1885 to discuss political issues
Early leaders known as Moderates demanded reforms through peaceful methods
The Swadeshi Movement encouraged people to boycott foreign goods and use Indian products
Mahatma Gandhi introduced mass movements based on nonviolence and truth
India finally achieved independence from British rule in 1947
examples: Example of Swadeshi: People boycotted British cloth and used hand-spun khadi
Example of Satyagraha: Peaceful protests against unjust laws
Example of Civil Disobedience: Breaking the salt law during the Salt March
Example of National Unity: People from different regions joining freedom movements
important_terms: Nationalism – Feeling of unity and loyalty towards the nation
Swadeshi – Use of goods produced in one's own country
Satyagraha – Nonviolent resistance based on truth
Boycott – Refusing to use or buy certain goods as a form of protest
exam_tips: Remember important years such as 1885 (formation of INC) and 1947 (independence)
Understand the difference between Moderates and Extremists
Learn key movements like Swadeshi Movement, Non-Cooperation Movement, and Civil Disobedience Movement
Write answers using important keywords like Swaraj, Swadeshi, and Satyagraha
Explain events with simple causes and effects in exams
